coverage_boundary: Rows prove that historically recurring FlowPilot control-plane failures are represented as parent gates over child fake-AI, historical replay, runtime, install-sync, current-transcript, and background-evidence checks. Each row is also promoted to a FlowGuard defect-family gate consumed by the final risk ledger. They do not prove arbitrary live AI semantic quality or unbounded production stress.

rows.defect_family_authority_boundary: role_output_runtime + packet_runtime + material_scan PM disposition writer
rows.expected_safe_behavior: PM disposition records rework without reviewer release, status names the failed self-check, and repair continues through fresh packet generation.
rows.full_confidence_boundary: Covers missing mechanical self-check fields and PM package disposition behavior; does not judge live AI semantic quality.
rows.historical_bad_case: Worker material-scan result body has a Contract Self-Check heading but misses required machine fields.
rows.runtime_surface: role_output_runtime + packet_runtime + material_scan PM disposition writer
rows.trigger_state: material_scan batch is results_relayed_to_pm and PM must decide whether every worker result satisfies the source output contract.

rows.defect_family_authority_boundary: control_blocker PM repair decision handler + persistent router daemon status projection
rows.expected_safe_behavior: Repair transaction, blocker index, decision flag, and daemon-visible next action are committed as one post-decision boundary.
rows.full_confidence_boundary: Covers executable repair waits and daemon-visible state for selected interleavings; does not prove an unbounded daemon soak.
rows.historical_bad_case: PM repair decision writes allowed follow-up events before daemon-visible state can see the PM repair decision flag.
rows.runtime_surface: control_blocker PM repair decision handler + persistent router daemon status projection
rows.trigger_state: control blocker requires PM repair and Router commits a follow-up wait or recheck event.

rows.defect_family_authority_boundary: scoped event identity replay + role-output ledger reconciliation + daemon durable-wait reconciliation
rows.expected_safe_behavior: Router classifies the stale conflict, preserves the legal wait or canonical package body, writes audit evidence, never accepts the stale body as success, and keeps the daemon live.
rows.full_confidence_boundary: Covers known repair-owned and stale-unowned package-disposition body_hash replay branches, including the foreground/daemon interleaving that previously recurred; does not prove unbounded daemon soak or live AI semantic quality without fresh historical replay and daemon artifacts.
rows.historical_bad_case: A daemon role-output ledger replay sees the same PM package disposition identity with a different body_hash after either a blocker/PM repair owns the conflict or a newer foreground PM body is already canonical.
rows.runtime_surface: scoped event identity replay + role-output ledger reconciliation + daemon durable-wait reconciliation
rows.trigger_state: A PM package disposition for the same batch/generation is already recorded, a stale conflicting role-output envelope remains replayable, and authority already points at either a blocker/repair owner or the current canonical package artifact.

rows.defect_family_authority_boundary: material packet generation registry + Router next-action projection
rows.expected_safe_behavior: Router exposes fresh producer evidence and only relays or waits on current-generation material work.
rows.full_confidence_boundary: Covers current-generation producer proof and stale-result rejection for bounded repair paths; does not prove every packet family.
rows.historical_bad_case: A packet_reissue repair leaves stale PM decision wording or waits for an event without a fresh packet producer.
rows.runtime_surface: material packet generation registry + Router next-action projection
rows.trigger_state: material repair requires reissuing worker packets after a rejected or rework-requested result.

rows.defect_family_authority_boundary: current status summary + route frontier display projection
rows.expected_safe_behavior: User-visible status derives from current Router facts and explicitly treats display as projection, not authority.
rows.full_confidence_boundary: Covers stale projection and current-pointer authority; does not prove every UI rendering path.
rows.historical_bad_case: Current status or display projection says a stale blocker, old ACK wait, or terminal-looking state while Router facts disagree.
rows.runtime_surface: current status summary + route frontier display projection
rows.trigger_state: Router has resolved ACK or PM repair facts but status summary is regenerated from old projection state.

rows.defect_family_authority_boundary: card ACK return ledger + Router ACK/return preconsumption
rows.expected_safe_behavior: ACK wait settlement and output-work completion remain separate obligations with separate status text and replay evidence.
rows.full_confidence_boundary: Covers ACK/return separation for known receipt paths; does not complete semantic role work.
rows.historical_bad_case: ACK-only receipt clearance reappears as a missing ACK blocker or is mistaken for semantic role output completion.
rows.runtime_surface: card ACK return ledger + Router ACK/return preconsumption
rows.trigger_state: Controller/Router return ledger has a valid ACK while the semantic role-output obligation remains pending.

rows.defect_family_authority_boundary: lifecycle stop request + daemon status + current pointer reconciliation
rows.expected_safe_behavior: Stop reconciles current pointer, run lifecycle, daemon status, heartbeat/manual-resume, pending Controller actions, and role continuation authority together.
rows.full_confidence_boundary: Covers controlled stop reconciliation for current pointer, daemon, and pending work authority; does not prove external scheduler delivery.
rows.historical_bad_case: A controlled stop releases daemon state but current pointer, pending Controller action, heartbeat, or role continuation still looks active.
rows.runtime_surface: lifecycle stop request + daemon status + current pointer reconciliation
rows.trigger_state: User stop/cancel or daemon release races current-run lifecycle projection and resume logic.
